name: Nimorith, the Gray Benefactor
description:
Nimorith, the Gray Benefactor

*{Lore Nimorith is a Trow patron, also answered by threshold-keepers, archivists of omissions, and oathwrights who work in the space between Said and Meant. He dwells in places that almost were---thresholds that shift when unobserved, rooms that measure longer than memory, shadows that fall where no lamp was lit. He does not arrive, he is simply the pause you finally notice. Those who bargain with him learn that presence is a luxury, absence a currency, and the space between doors a kingdom unto itself. To serve him is to walk at the corner of sight, to speak in the breath held before truth is told.

quote "Take what is not there, and you shall have what never was. The gap is the gift; the missing, the mercy." quote

*{Domain Focus itemize Thresholds & Liminality: doors, gates, moments of transition, the unseen Absence & Omission: erasure, forgetting, silence, what is not said Memory & Witness: testimony, collateral, the price of truth Subtlety & Misdirection: stealth, deception by omission, unseen paths itemize

*{Thiasos and Codex (Runekeeper Options)

A Runekeeper sworn to Nimorith does not bind a familiar of flesh and blood. Their Thiasos is a creature of silence and gaps: a moth that flies only in the blind spot of the eye, a spider that weaves webs only in unlit corners, a mouse that is only ever heard, never seen, or a patch of shadow that moves against the light. This creature requires no food, but it must be offered a silence daily---a minute of absolute stillness and quiet contemplation from its bearer. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (it fades, imposing --1 die on all Stealth or Subterfuge rolls for its bearer until a significant silence is given).

The Codex of Nimorith is never a whole book. It may be a collection of blank vellum pages that reveal writing only when held in a specific type of shadow, a slate that must be dusted with ash from an extinguished lamp, or a key that unlocks a non-existent drawer. Because Nimorith's truths are written in omissions, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week in a truly liminal space (a doorway at twilight, a forgotten attic, a crossroads) and erase one of their own memories onto its pages, or the symbols fade (treat as Compromised until a suitable omission is made).

*{Patron's Gift (Imbuement)

Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of Nimorith may focus on a specific absence---a missing object, a silent moment, an unlit lamp. For the rest of the scene, they gain +1 die to Stealth or Subterfuge rolls that exploit that absence, and the first time each scene they would fail to move unseen through a threshold, they may reroll the check (the shadows part for them). After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to Nimorith.
summary: Nimorith, the Gray Benefactor *{Lore Nimorith is a Trow patron, also answered by threshold-keepers, archivists of omissions, and oathwrights who work in the space between Said and Meant.
